title: U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day, 2026
summary: Proclamation 11016 of March 9, 2026, declares March 9, 2026, as U.S. Hostage and Wrongful Detainee Day. The proclamation highlights the administration's success in securing the release of 101 detained Americans abroad in the past year, including brokering a peace deal between Israel and Gaza for hostage release, securing the release of Americans from Venezuela, and freeing hostages from Afghanistan, Russia, and Belarus. It also specifically addresses Iran's history of detaining Americans as leverage and announces a new Executive Order to combat wrongful detentions abroad. This order allows the designation of countries as 'State Sponsors of Wrongful Detention' and imposes retaliatory measures such as sanctions. Iran has been the first country designated under this authority. The proclamation reaffirms the commitment to bringing all Americans home and defending American lives and interests through a policy of "peace through strength."
